item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The bit about "there are two people in this room" was great, but the rest of this is a poorly realized mix of things other articles have done much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was an error. Mistopheles is a stupid name for a doctor, admittedly, but i was stuck on what to change it to instead. it was originally going to be ramirez, but then i changed it to Mathis. Unfortunately, it seems I did a poor job of it.

item.title: Re: SCP 4539 - The Worst Calculator Ever
item.content:encoded: I edited a draft of this, and I'm so happy it was finally released! Updoot from me. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Confused by the Ramirez/Mathis interchangeability, think it was an error but not confident enough to change it.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Oh, sorry! The images were taken by me. I'll include that in my first post.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The images used in this article are currently unsourced, and therefore may be a violation of the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). If you can provide the source for the images, then I will determine whether they are cc-compliant or not. If they are, then the images will stay. If they aren't, the images will go.

EDIT: The images have been sourced and are cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually think this is a really cool concept for a scp, I would even say it's one of my favourite concepts. Though I think the writing is a bit "off" for scp standards, so I think it would be best to slightly change the dialogue a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are a few things very awkward about this one, especially the dialogue and the ending (which aismallard has already commented on,) but this is a very strong concept. The Foundation's reliance on math and numbers is something I never really considered, and asking what happens when those things are taken away is incredibly compelling.

Upvoted for the concept alone and the strong first half of the article. Tinker with the dialogue and change the ending a bit, and this will be an overall great SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's kind of janky in places, and "mistopheles" as a person's name seems kind of goofy, but overall I like the narrative well enough to upvote. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has potential, and I want to see it realized.

I really liked the attention to containment (you end up realizing how much the Foundation relies on numbering things), but the narrative progression slowly falls apart from there. The test does not do enough to escalate the story, and the interview, well it sort of falls in the same vein of stepping things up a bit less than what is needed.

I didn't really buy the ending containment breach/interview at all. It didn't feel like a continuous progression of a prior story thread, and the ending was not satisfying or compelling to me.

I get how you feel, with my first draft I also struggled with many revisions and finding a way to land it. Transitioning from thinking about tests as tests to thinking of the article as a story is a difficult one to make.

Overall, this would normally be a downvote but I particularly liked the concept so it's a neutralvote.

item.title: SCP 4539 - The Worst Calculator Ever
item.content:encoded:
Finally, after no less than 90 revisions, this infernal SCP is OUTIE FIVE-THOUTIE!

Some things:

D, E, C, and I are the fourth, fifth, third, and ninth letters in the alphabet. That's why it's alt designation is DECI. rules permitting, i'd apply that DECI across the entire page and stuff, but that's easier said than done.

DECI and MALinda are just two arbitrarily chosen names, no hidden number pun or anything.

My thanks to:

TheBlueHour and DrMorris: for seeing the potential the idea had and greenlighting it.
Kyza: For inspiring me to try my hand at HTML Formatting.
ihp, AlanDaris, RTME, and many others like JakDragonX, sigkreiger, Balee, and probably a dozen other people: for suffering through the many loooooong drafts this skip had to review it, and slowly polish it to a shine. Thank you, everyone, for all your help - I couldn't have done it without you!

Both images were taken by me, and I'm willing to release them as being under the site's license.

EDIT: ooof, these comments sting a bit. The general gist I'm getting is that it's a good concept held back a bit by my writing skills.

For now, I'm focused on getting three articles. The next one I'm making is nearly complete, and the third one has the first part done. it's generally garnered a more positive reception, so who knows?

This skip needs more love in the writing, but for now, I'm going to leave it mostly as-is. I'll come back to it once I'm a better writer than I am now. Thank you for all your feedback!
